2	  "body": "It came up in discussion with @cmungall  that currently there is no disjunction between multicellular organism and organism substance.  Since NCBITaxon terms correspond to multicellular organisms, someone can define a 'human milk'[edit] as an axiom like:\r\n\r\n'human milk': equivalentTo 'milk and 'Homo Sapiens'\r\n\r\nand not get a logical contradiction.\r\n\r\n**Suggested revision of logical definition**\r\n\r\nCan we make multicellular organism and organism substance disjoint to trigger unsatisfiability?\r\n",
3	  "comments": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd85CcDzn",
6	      "author": {
7	        "login": "shawntanzk"
8	      },
9	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
10	      "body": "added to uberon agenda, will assign you",
11	      "createdAt": "2022-05-02T09:15:19Z",
12	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
13	      "isMinimized": false,
14	      "minimizedReason": "",
15	      "reactionGroups": [],
16	      "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/2421#issuecomment-1114651879",
17	      "viewerDidAuthor": false
18	    },
19	    {
20	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd85FmNI5",
21	      "author": {
22	        "login": ""
23	      },
24	      "authorAssociation": "NONE",
25	      "body": "@ddooley, can you advise what is the ultimate goal that you would like to achieve? By making disjoint as you mention above, there is concern this would cause downstream issues. There are concerns with scope related to food (FoodOn) vs anatomy terms (Uberon).",
26	      "createdAt": "2022-06-27T17:23:05Z",
27	      "includesCreatedEdit": true,
28	      "isMinimized": false,
29	      "minimizedReason": "",
30	      "reactionGroups": [],
31	      "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/2421#issuecomment-1167643193",
32	      "viewerDidAuthor": false
33	    },
34	    {
35	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd85Fmayj",
36	      "author": {
37	        "login": "ddooley"
38	      },
39	      "authorAssociation": "NONE",
40	      "body": "I think it's a basic quality control check - a distinction between a whole, and a proper part.  We need to know which references point to whole organisms, and which do not. One should not be confused with the other, so this would trip an error if that happened in UBERON or someone's reuse of it.\r\n\r\nHowever, do things get complicated if, as Chris told me, a reference to an UBERON part, such as heart, logically entails connection to some whole organism in an abstract (dead or alive) sense? I really need an answer to that question - in FoodOn we need to be able to talk about a separated (severed / butchered) heart, one that does not entail a whole connected organism.\r\n",
41	      "createdAt": "2022-06-27T18:08:15Z",
42	      "includesCreatedEdit": false,
43	      "isMinimized": false,
44	      "minimizedReason": "",
45	      "reactionGroups": [],
46	      "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/2421#issuecomment-1167699107",
47	      "viewerDidAuthor": false
48	    },
49	    {
50	      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd85Fmsd7",
51	      "author": {
52	        "login": "cmungall"
53	      },
54	      "authorAssociation": "MEMBER",
55	      "body": "Let's keep this issues separate\r\n\r\nThis issue is about the request to make substance and organism disjoint. This is completely reasonable and straightforward. I am surprised we don't have this already, actually.\r\n\r\nI don't think there are any concerns. I can't think of any exceptions. there are no organisms made of gas, liquid or other substances created by the body. even an ingested animal ceases at some point to become a multicellular organism and is broken down into substances.\r\n\r\nNote that X disjoint with Y simply means there is nothing that instantiates X and Y. There are no further entailments to do with connectedness or parthood.\r\n\r\nWe already have other issues for the butchered heart use case, but that is completely separate. I think we should grant this request, close this issue, and continue discussing butchered hearts on the other tickets",
56	      "createdAt": "2022-06-27T19:11:04Z",

2	ontology: uberon/external-disjoints
3	property_value: dc-title "Uberon external disjoints" xsd:string
4	property_value: dc-description "This ontology contains disjointness axioms between Uberon classes and classes from external ontologies (mostly anatomy ontologies). Use of this ontology can help prevent accidental associations between classes which may on the surface seem similar or equivalent (for example, based on the term) but are in fact completely distinct. This ontology can be used on combination with an OWL reasoner. For more details, see the homepage of this ontology - http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/docs/Inter-ontology-disjointness-Design-Pattern" xsd:string

ontology: uberon/external-disjoints
property_value: dc-title "Uberon external disjoints" xsd:string
property_value: dc-description "This ontology contains disjointness axioms between Uberon classes and classes from external ontologies (mostly anatomy ontologies). Use of this ontology can help prevent accidental associations between classes which may on the surface seem similar or equivalent (for example, based on the term) but are in fact completely distinct. This ontology can be used on combination with an OWL reasoner. For more details, see the homepage of this ontology - http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/docs/Inter-ontology-disjointness-Design-Pattern" xsd:string
property_value: dc-creator "Chris Mungall" xsd:string

property_value: seeAlso https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/55

ontology: uberon/pending-disjoints

For more details, see the homepage of this ontology - http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/docs/Inter-ontology-disjointness-Design-Pattern" xsd:string
property_value: dc-creator "Chris Mungall" xsd:string
property_value: foaf-homepage "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/docs/Inter-ontology-disjointness-Design-Pattern" xsd:anyURI
idspace: NIF_GrossAnatomy http://ontology.neuinfo.org/NIF/BiomaterialEntities/NIF-GrossAnatomy.owl#

synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"

synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"

subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"

subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""

subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"

Make organism substance disjoint from multicellular organism (#2421)

Adds an explicit disjoint_from axiom between organism substance
(UBERON:0000463) and multicellular organism (UBERON:0000468), as
requested in issue #2421.

Without this axiom, definitions such as 'human milk' equivalentTo
'milk and Homo sapiens' do not yield a logical contradiction, even
though a substance produced by an organism is not itself a whole
multicellular organism.

While this disjointness was previously entailed via the disjoint
already present on UBERON:0000001 (gross anatomical part), making it
explicit at the substance/organism level makes the constraint more
discoverable and robust for downstream ontologies and reusers.

Signed-off-by: @dragon-ai-agent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
# Make organism substance disjoint from multicellular organism

Addresses issue #2421.

## Change

Added a single line to `UBERON:0000463` (`organism substance`) in `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo`:

```
disjoint_from: UBERON:0000468 {source="https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/2421"} ! multicellular organism
```

## Rationale

The issue (raised by @ddooley and seconded by @cmungall) requests that `multicellular organism` and `organism substance` be made disjoint, so that mistakenly defining e.g. `human milk` as `milk and Homo sapiens` (where `Homo sapiens` is treated as a subclass of multicellular organism) triggers unsatisfiability instead of silently being accepted.

In @cmungall's words on the issue:

> This issue is about the request to make substance and organism disjoint. This is completely reasonable and straightforward. I am surprised we don't have this already, actually. I can't think of any exceptions. There are no organisms made of gas, liquid or other substances created by the body. Even an ingested animal ceases at some point to become a multicellular organism and is broken down into substances.

## Validation checklist

- [x] Verified both terms exist with the expected IDs:
  - `UBERON:0000463` ! organism substance
  - `UBERON:0000468` ! multicellular organism
- [x] Confirmed no existing direct `disjoint_from` between them in `uberon-edit.obo`, `external-disjoints.obo`, or `pending-disjoints.obo`.
- [x] Noted that `UBERON:0000001` (gross anatomical part — parent of organism substance) is already `disjoint_from UBERON:0000468`, so the new axiom is consistent with the existing logical commitments and the disjointness was in fact already entailed via subclass. Adding the explicit axiom at the substance/organism level makes the constraint locally discoverable for downstream ontologies and reusers (e.g. FoodOn) and does not depend on importing `gross anatomical part`.
- [x] Checked that the existing `relationship: part_of UBERON:0000468` on `organism substance` does not conflict: in OWL, `disjoint_from` constrains class-level instance overlap, while `part_of` is a relation between instances of (potentially distinct) classes — a substance can be part of an organism without being one. The existing editor note already flags that the `part_of` may be too strong; this PR does not change that.
- [x] Placed the axiom in `uberon-edit.obo` rather than `external-disjoints.obo`. The latter is documented as being for "disjointness axioms between Uberon classes and classes from external ontologies", whereas this is an internal UBERON-UBERON disjoint and follows the same pattern already used on `UBERON:0000001`.
- [x] Did not place in `pending-disjoints.obo`, which is reserved for disjoints that would produce unsatisfiable classes in the production release. No unsatisfiability is expected from this change, per the issue discussion.
- [x] Used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl` workflow per `CLAUDE.md` and reserialised the edit file with `robot convert`.

## Files changed

- `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo` — single line added on the `organism substance` term.

Signed-off-by: @dragon-ai-agent

  - `UBERON:0000463` ! organism substance
  - `UBERON:0000468` ! multicellular organism
- [x] Confirmed no existing direct `disjoint_from` between them in `uberon-edit.obo`, `external-disjoints.obo`, or `pending-disjoints.obo`.
- [x] Noted that `UBERON:0000001` (gross anatomical part — parent of organism substance) is already `disjoint_from UBERON:0000468`, so the new axiom is consistent with the existing logical commitments and the disjointness was in fact already entailed via subclass. Adding the explicit axiom at the substance/organism level makes the constraint locally discoverable for downstream ontologies and reusers (e.g. FoodOn) and does not depend on importing `gross anatomical part`.
- [x] Checked that the existing `relationship: part_of UBERON:0000468` on `organism substance` does not conflict: in OWL, `disjoint_from` constrains class-level instance overlap, while `part_of` is a relation between instances of (potentially distinct) classes — a substance can be part of an organism without being one. The existing editor note already flags that the `part_of` may be too strong; this PR does not change that.
- [x] Placed the axiom in `uberon-edit.obo` rather than `external-disjoints.obo`. The latter is documented as being for "disjointness axioms between Uberon classes and classes from external ontologies", whereas this is an internal UBERON-UBERON disjoint and follows the same pattern already used on `UBERON:0000001`.
- [x] Did not place in `pending-disjoints.obo`, which is reserved for disjoints that would produce unsatisfiable classes in the production release. No unsatisfiability is expected from this change, per the issue discussion.
- [x] Used `obo-checkout.pl` / `obo-checkin.pl` workflow per `CLAUDE.md` and reserialised the edit file with `robot convert`.

## Files changed

- `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo` — single line added on the `organism substance` term.
